justification: All pass criteria met: FulfillmentWorker Kafka trace break clearly identified as the primary issue; NotificationService and AuditService missing trace context both identified with specific interaction analysis; consequence explicitly named (on-call engineer must manually correlate timestamps across multiple log streams, described as 'effectively impossible' at scale); cross-service SLO gap identified with specific examples (end-to-end order-to-fulfillment latency, orders created vs fulfilled vs notified); specific fix recommendations provided for each trace break (extract trace ID from Kafka headers, propagate X-Trace-ID on all HTTP calls, OpenTelemetry collector); verdict is NOT READY.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough interaction-by-interaction analysis covering all six service boundaries with correct status assessments
Consequence analysis is vivid and operationally grounded — describes the exact 3am debugging workflow an on-call engineer would face
Identifies the 40%/60% traced vs dark split, quantifying the observability gap
Goes beyond rubric requirements with resilience assessment covering consumer lag, silent drops, and DLQ correlation
Escalations are well-structured with clear routing and actionable next steps
Notes the AuditService dual-caller problem — both OrderService and FulfillmentWorker call it without trace context, compounding the correlation problem
Identifies missing business-level reconciliation metrics (orders published vs consumed) as a silent failure detection gap

agent_output_excerpt: {"integration_assessment":{"component_interactions":[{"from":"API Gateway","to":"OrderService","status":"correct","issue":"Trace ID propagated correctly via X-Trace-ID header; structured logs correlate."},{"from":"OrderService","to":"Kafka","status":"correct","issue":"Trace ID included in Kafka message headers; publish side is correct."},{"from":"Kafka","to":"FulfillmentWorker","status":"broken","issue":"FulfillmentWorker does NOT extract trace ID from Kafka message headers.
